name: Fairmont Field Golf Course
about: The Fairmont Field Club is a historic country club founded in 1912, located in Fairmont, West Virginia. It features a challenging 9-hole golf course, a clubhouse, and the 1912 Tavern. The club offers both public and member play, along with amenities like golf simulators, pickleball, a pool, and event spaces. It is known for its tight fairways, undulating greens, and comprehensive golf programs, including lessons and leagues for all skill levels.
why_golfers_like_it: 3 high-quality golf simulators for practice and play
Comprehensive junior golf programs with over 200 kids
Event hosting capabilities for indoor and outdoor spaces

food_and_drink.notes: On-site restaurant (1912 Tavern) available for dining and event catering
Alcohol service available at the 1912 Tavern and events

simulator_hardware.notes: 3 full simulator bays available, specific hardware not detailed

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The club does not specify club rental availability, so it is recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The club has an on-site restaurant (1912 Tavern) and catering services, so outside food and drinks may not be permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The course is a 9-hole layout, so playing 18 holes would typically take 2-3 hours depending on pace of play.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the club offers junior golf programs, leagues, and camps for kids, making it a family-friendly venue.
